item.title: Performance budget and Lighthouse validation
item.intent: Every page must score 95+ on all four Lighthouse categories (Performance, Accessibility, Best Practices, SEO). Zero layout shift, sub-100KB page weight for content pages, no render-blocking resources. The site plan says fast load — this enforces it.
item.description: Run Lighthouse CI on key pages post-build. Fix any issues found. Establish a baseline and optionally add to CI. Requires the home page to exist first.
steps: Make Google Fonts non-render-blocking in src/layouts/Base.astro: replace the <link rel="stylesheet" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/..."> with a <link rel="preload" as="style" ...> plus an onload handler to swap it to rel="stylesheet", with a <noscript> fallback. Keep the existing preconnect hints.
Verify dist/index.html exists and is under 100KB by running npm run build and checking file size with stat.
Verify no synchronous (render-blocking) font stylesheet remains in the built HTML by confirming there is no <link rel="stylesheet" ... href containing fonts.googleapis in dist/index.html.
Add an npm script named "check:perf" to package.json that runs: npm run build && stat -c%s dist/index.html | awk '{exit ($1 >= 102400)}' — gives a repeatable way to validate page weight.

acceptanceCriteria.verify: npm run build && SIZE=$(stat -c%s dist/index.html 2>/dev/null || stat -f%z dist/index.html) && test "$SIZE" -lt 102400

acceptanceCriteria.text: No render-blocking Google Fonts stylesheet tag in built home page (preconnects and preloads are fine; only synchronous rel=stylesheet is blocked)
acceptanceCriteria.verify: npm run build && ! grep -qP '<link[^>]+rel=["\x27]stylesheet["\x27][^>]+href=["\x27]https://fonts\.googleapis' dist/index.html && ! grep -qP '<link[^>]+href=["\x27]https://fonts\.googleapis[^>]+rel=["\x27]stylesheet["\x27]' dist/index.html

acceptanceCriteria.text: check:perf npm script exists in package.json
